digests for historical days. Respects the input-hash skip optimisation —
set a different day_number or pass fresh data to force a re-run.

post.description:
Manually trigger the trip-level summary digest.

Normally fires automatically when trip status → completed, but this
endpoint lets you run it ad-hoc — e.g., after adjusting the summary
prompt or to inspect in-progress synthesis.

get.description: Get the trip-level summary digest for a trip, if one exists.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Summary Digest Admin Trips  Trip Id  Digest Summary Get

schema.title: Response Get Digest Admin Digests  Digest Id  Get

post.description:
Manually trigger cross-day planning-context synthesis for a trip.

Normally auto-fires at the end of each daily digest; this endpoint lets
you re-run it ad-hoc after tuning the synthesis prompt or inspect the
current output without waiting for the next daily run.

get.description: Get the current planning-context digest for a trip, if one exists.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Planning Context Admin Trips  Trip Id  Digest Planning Context Get

post.description:
Re-run daily digests for a targeted list of trips.

Designed for outage recovery: when the background digest loop logs
``failed_trip_ids``, paste those IDs here to re-run without waiting
for the next 6-hour cycle.

Each trip's ``day_number`` is computed from its ``start_date`` and
the target date.  Trips with no ``start_date``, or where the target
date falls outside ``[start_date, end_date]``, are skipped with
status ``no_date``.

The endpoint is idempotent — ``generate_daily_digest`` respects its
input-hash skip optimisation, so re-running a trip that already has
an up-to-date digest returns ``status="skipped"`` without billable LLM
calls.

get.description:
Engagement statistics for a trip's proactive notification outcomes.

Useful for evaluating how well the triage agent is targeting notifications
— low response rates on ``time_sensitive`` or ``action_needed`` urgency
are a signal that the LLM is over-notifying or picking the wrong channel.

Returns:
- Overall response rate across all recorded outcomes
- Average response window in minutes for outcomes that received a reply
- Per-urgency breakdown (response rate by urgency tier)
- Per-outcome distribution (achieved / not_achieved / pending / …)
- Top-10 intents sorted by response count

Report ingestion provider configuration and per-source experience counts.

Returns which providers are configured (have valid credentials),
a count of experiences per source, and the most recent ingested_at
timestamp for each source.

Return all angles with status='pending_review', newest first.

Optionally filter to a single place by passing `place_slug`.
No pagination — this is a curator tool intended for dev/admin use.

schema.title: Response List Pending Angles Admin Angles Pending Get

post.description:
Approve a pending angle, moving it to status='queued'.

Sets `reviewed_at` to the current server time and optionally records
`review_notes`. Returns the angle row as it was at the moment of approval
(status field is omitted from the response model since it's no longer
pending_review after this call).

Raises 404 if the angle doesn't exist or is not in `pending_review`.

Reject a pending angle, moving it to status='rejected'.

Sets `reviewed_at` to the current server time and records the required
`review_notes`. Returns the angle row as it was at the moment of rejection
(status field is omitted from the response model since it's no longer
pending_review after this call).

Raises 404 if the angle doesn't exist or is not in `pending_review`.

get.description:
List published angles for a place, ordered by priority + distinctiveness.

Passing ``user_id`` overlays per-user personalization: each angle
picks up a ``why_for_you`` hook and ``personalization_rank`` from
the reader's Personal Memory, and the list is reordered by that
rank. Anonymous callers (no ``user_id``) get the editorial order.

Editorial index — published angles grouped by travel lens.

The ordering within each group follows priority → distinctiveness (same
as the flat list endpoint). Groups are ordered by the canonical lens
taxonomy: why_here, day_in, affinity, insider, tension, ritual.
``unlensed`` captures any published angles that predate the lens system.

Generate speculative sub-angles on the fly from a user query.

Returns 3-5 ephemeral angle suggestions that are complementary to what
already exists for the place. These are never persisted — they are
fast-start discovery chips for the browse surface.

On failure (LLM error, empty place), returns an empty ``suggestions`` list.
Full angle detail — dossier body + member venues with Take headlines.
Angles this venue is a member of. Powers the breadcrumb chip.

post.description:
Send a message to the concierge for this trip.

Delegates to the DB-backed conversation session. Creates the trip's
group conversation on first call.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries: a second
call with the same key returns the cached response (if the original
completed) or 409 Conflict (if still processing).

Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries. A repeat
request with the same key whose original is still streaming returns
HTTP 409 (before the SSE stream begins); a repeat whose original
completed returns the cached response as a single metadata event.

get.description:
Get conversation history for this trip from the DB.

Uses ``get_or_create_trip_conversation`` so the response always
carries ``conversation_id`` — even on the first load before any
messages exist. The client uses this ID to target the SSE stream
endpoint (``POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages/stream``) without
a second round-trip to discover the conversation.

schema.title: Response Get History Api Trips  Trip Id  Messages History Get

get.description: Get basic session info (turn count, message count).

Triggers post-session reflection for all participants.

schema.title: Response Close Session Endpoint Api Trips  Trip Id  Messages Close Post

post.description:
Get or create the private 1:1 conversation for this user + trip.

Allows a user to have a private chat with the concierge about
trip-specific topics (budget concerns, social dynamics, personal
preferences) without the group seeing the conversation.

Idempotent — safe to call on every Concierge tab mount. Returns the
existing conversation if one already exists for this (user, trip) pair.
The agent has full access to trip context but skips group coordination
features (compose_group_message, strict_group_compose, privacy stripping).

to hide a destination.

post.description:
Generate a spoken-monologue narration for a place the user has arrived at.

The Concierge runs in narration mode: persona slot in IDENTITY_CORE
(auto-resolved from the trip's destination — Mateus for Lisbon,
Keiko for Tokyo, etc.) plus ``SKILL_GUIDE``. Single-shot — no agent
loop, no tool calls.

Pass ``X-Idempotency-Key`` to deduplicate retries (mobile network is
flaky on geofence triggers; without a key, each retry burns a fresh
Sonnet call). Re-narration on the same place uses a fresh key per
user tap.
Create a new conversation (group or personal).

get.description: Get a conversation with its participants.
Get paginated message history for a conversation.

schema.title: Response Get Messages Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Get

post.description:
Send a message in a conversation.

If ``trigger_agent`` is True (default), the concierge processes the
message and returns its reply. If False, the message is persisted as a
user-to-user message and no agent reply is generated — useful for group
chat where users want to talk to each other.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries (only
applies when ``trigger_agent`` is True; user-to-user messages don't
need retry protection at this layer).

schema.title: Response Send Message Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Post

post.description:
Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

If ``trigger_agent`` is False, returns a single SSE event with the
persisted message ID (no streaming needed).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``user_message``: when trigger_agent=False, the persisted message.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Stamp the conversation's ``last_location`` with a fresh reading.

Participants only — the conversation's GPS is private to its
members. Mobile clients piggyback location on message sends for
the common path; this endpoint exists for background pushes
(foreground event, significant-change updates) where there's no
message to attach to.

Idempotent-ish: latest write wins. Readings older than 15 minutes
are filtered at read time by the origin resolver, so stale pushes
don't pollute — but callers shouldn't push stale readings anyway.

Promote a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

Reads the conversation's ``intent_state`` scratchpad, creates a trip
row with best-effort field mapping (title / place_id / dates),
links them both ways, copies conversation participants as trip
members, and stamps ``intent_state.phase='committed'``.

Only the conversation creator may promote — the resulting trip is
owned by that user (``trip.created_by = conversation.created_by``)
so allowing anyone else to drive this would let an attacker spawn
trips under a victim's name. The rest of the conversations router
is unauthenticated (house style for a separate reason), but
promotion is uniquely destructive and earns its gate.

Fails with:
- 403 if the caller isn't the conversation creator
- 400 on not-found / home / already-promoted / not-ready

get.description:
List all conversations for a user, enriched for history rendering.

Returns ``last_message_preview`` (≤140 chars), ``last_message_at``,
``last_message_sender``, ``last_message_modality``, and
``unread_count`` per row so the client can render a useful list
without follow-up fetches.

schema.title: Response List User Conversations Api Users  User Id  Conversations Get

post.description:
Log a single user event.

Called by the frontend when a user performs an action (vote, view,
edit, etc.). Events are read by async pipelines via cursors.

When ``event_type == 'discover_session_end'``, a background task fires to
synthesize the session's Discover signals into Personal Memory observations.
The ``context`` field should contain ``session_start`` (ISO-8601 string) so
the synthesizer knows how far back to look.

Log multiple user events at once.

Useful for batching UI events or replaying actions. If any event in the
batch is ``discover_session_end``, synthesis is triggered once (using the
earliest ``session_start`` found in that event's context).

Decide voice / text / push / defer / silent for a proactive turn.

Auth: only the user themselves can request a routing for their

get.description:
Return navigable itinerary stops for today (or a given date) with geofence metadata.

Used by the mobile geofence hook (``useNarrationGeofence``). Each stop carries:
  - entity coordinates (lat/lng) from the venue or site geometry
  - radius_m: geofence trigger radius (150 m for all stops)
  - narrated: whether the user has already narrated this entity on this trip
    (derived from ``message_type='narration'`` rows in their personal
    trip conversation — Phase 5 source-of-truth migration)

Only venue/site blocks are returned (free_time and transit blocks are skipped).
Stops are returned in day/sort_order sequence.

parameters.name: date

schema.description: Return stops for this calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today. Falls back to all itinerary stops when no days have a date set (e.g. demo/test itineraries).
schema.title: Date

parameters.description: Return stops for this calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today. Falls back to all itinerary stops when no days have a date set (e.g. demo/test itineraries).

schema.title: Response Guide Stops Api Trips  Trip Id  Narration Stops Get

post.description:
Speculative pre-render hint — fires LLM + TTS before geofence dwell expires.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.4. Called by the client when the user is
approaching a known stop (within ~200 m and walking toward it).
The endpoint is a thin wrapper that:

1. Verifies the actor is a trip member (existing auth).
2. Verifies the requested stop is part of THIS trip (avoids
   arbitrary speculative renders for stops the user isn't on).
3. Checks if audio for this stop is already cached → no-op return.
4. Otherwise enqueues ``render_intros_for_user_trip`` for the
   actor (the trip-level fan-out is idempotent; the worker skips
   already-rendered stops).

Idempotent and racy-safe — multiple clients triggering the same
hint within the cache TTL all converge on the same cached audio,
so over-firing is cheap. The endpoint accepts a single
``(entity_type, entity_id)`` rather than a list to keep the client
simple; if the user is approaching multiple stops, the client
fires multiple hints in parallel.

get.description:
Return the offline-narration manifest for the actor on this trip.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.5. The client fetches this once on WiFi
pre-departure, batch-downloads every audio entry to local storage,
and plays from the local path while offline / on expensive data.

Each ``audio_url`` is relative to the API base — clients construct
the absolute URL via their own configured BASE_URL. Re-fetching the
manifest after additional renders complete returns a superset of
the previous response (idempotent + monotonic).

The ``ready_count`` and ``pending_count`` let the client surface a
"ready for offline" indicator. When ``pending_count > 0``, the
client may choose to defer the offline-bundle download and re-poll
the manifest after a short delay.

Return pre-rendered MP3 audio for a stop, or 404 if not yet cached.

Used by ``useNarrationAudio`` on the mobile client to play narration audio
when available. The audio cache is populated by the prerender pipeline
(``backend/guide/prerender.py``) which fires on ``itinerary.committed``
events. Cache miss is normal — the chat narration card silently
omits the play button when audio isn't ready.

post.description:
Attempt to claim the narration lease on (trip, entity).

Idempotent — a self-claim returns the existing lease without
changes. A losing claim returns the active holder's info so the
UI can render the join affordance.
Release a lease the caller holds. Idempotent (no-op when not the holder).
Extend the lease's TTL — used when narration runs longer than expected.

get.description:
Peek at the active lease for an entity (for the join affordance).

Returns null when no one is currently narrating.

schema.title: Response Get Active Narration Lease Api Trips  Trip Id  Narration Lease Active  Entity Type   Entity Id  Get

post.description:
Record one narration engagement event.

Append-only. Returns ``{recorded: false}`` (HTTP 200) for unknown
events so a typo on the client doesn't fail the request — the
invalid event is logged server-side.

get.description:
Return a list of privacy-sensitive signal-use events for ``user_id``.

Auth: only the user themselves can request their own audit log.
No admin override — a user's privacy report is for them alone.

Today the underlying source is an in-process aggregation.
Phase 8 graduates this to a real warehouse query. The API
contract here is the stable surface clients code against.

parameters.name: since

schema.description: ISO datetime — return events on or after this time. Defaults to 24 hours ago if omitted.
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: ISO datetime — return events on or after this time. Defaults to 24 hours ago if omitted.

post.description:
Mint a new invite token for a trip.

Gated on ``trip_has_substance`` — if the trip is a blank slate the
snapshot would be generic, which the Intake Philosophy doc is
explicit about avoiding (§6: generic first message permanently
poisons the relationship).

get.description:
List all invites for a trip. Organizer-only.

Side effect: stamps ``intake_seen_at = now()`` on every invite for the
trip, treating the act of opening the list as the organizer's
acknowledgement of any pending intake answers. This is what clears
the "Sam answered" pip in the notification bell — see
``NotificationFeed.pending_invite_answers``.

schema.title: Response List Invites Api Trips  Trip Id  Invites Get

delete.description: Revoke a trip invite. Organizer-only, idempotent.

post.description:
Mint an invite for a pre-trip conversation.

Gated on ``conversation_has_substance`` — mirrors the trip-invite
gate but reads from intent_state rather than trip briefs. Keeps us
from sending friends a landing page that says nothing about the
trip being shaped.

get.description: List all invites for a conversation. Creator-only.

schema.title: Response List Conversation Invites Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Invites Get

delete.description: Revoke a conversation invite. Creator-only, idempotent.

get.description:
Render the landing page for an invite token. Public endpoint.

Branches on scope: trip invites render the committed-trip summary
with a Haiku-generated snapshot (cached). Conversation invites
(Phase 4, pre-trip group chat) render a lightweight summary built
directly from intent_state — no LLM call, no caching needed, since
the substance bar is low and the content is already structured.

post.description:
Record pre-auth chip answers on the invite.

Public endpoint — the invitee hasn't signed up yet. The payload is
merged into the invite's pending_intake JSONB and handed to the
concierge at accept-time so the conversation picks up continuously.

Consume the invite and add the authenticated user as a member.

Branches on scope: trip invites add the user to trip_members and log
an ``invite.consumed`` trip_event. Conversation invites (Phase 4)
add the user to conversation_participants; on promotion those
participants are copied over to trip_members automatically (see
promotion.py). Event logging for conversation invites is deferred
— we don't have a conversation_events table yet and the audit gap
at this pre-trip stage is acceptable.
Handle a lookup query and return a fast answer.
Add a user to a trip. Only organizers may add members.

get.description: List all members of a trip.

schema.title: Response List Members Api Trips  Trip Id  Members Get

patch.description: Update a trip member's role. Only organizers may change roles.

delete.description:
Remove a user from a trip.

Members may remove themselves; organizers may remove anyone.

get.description: List people this user has traveled with, ordered by most recent shared trip.

schema.title: Response List Co Travelers Api Users  User Id  Co Travelers Get

post.description:
Record a user's response to a notification.

``response_type`` advances the outcome's state forward only
(pending → seen → acknowledged → engaged → acted).
Sending a weaker type than the current state is a no-op — returns
204 regardless so the client never needs to know the current state.

Typically called:
- ``seen`` — user dismissed the banner / opened the conversation
- ``engaged`` — user tapped the notification / replied in the chat
- ``acted`` — user completed the suggested action (e.g. booked a venue)

Resolve the evaluation outcome for a notification.

Records whether the notification's intent was ultimately achieved.
This is the authoritative resolution used by the triage scheduler
to learn which notification strategies are effective.

get.description: Get the user's global notification preferences (creates defaults if absent).

patch.description: Update one or more fields on the user's notification preferences.

get.description: Get the per-trip notification state (includes cadence_override).

patch.description:
Set (or clear) the per-trip cadence override for a specific user.

Only the user themselves or the trip organizer may call this.
Pass cadence_override=null to clear and inherit from the user's global preference.

get.description: List change proposals for a trip.

schema.title: Response List Proposals Api Trips  Trip Id  Proposals Get

get.description: Get full details of a change proposal.

post.description:
Cast a vote on a proposal.

The voter is the authenticated actor — ``user_id`` is no longer accepted
in the request body to prevent impersonation.

Resolve a proposal (accept, reject, supersede, or withdraw).

Only trip organizers can resolve. Members can contribute signal
through conversation; the organizer makes the call.

Revert an accepted proposal, restoring the itinerary to its prior state.

Only organizers may revert. The proposal status is set to ``withdrawn``
and a bridge message is posted to the group chat.

Kick off a quick research pass in the background.

The instant answer should have already been returned via POST /api/lookup/.
This endpoint triggers a short graph run (3 iterations, ~1 minute) that
produces a dossier and persists structured data.

Kick off a deep research pass in the background.

Runs the full research graph (up to 15 iterations by default) with quality
gate, dossier writing, and persistence. Use profile_overrides for
curation-level runs (e.g. {"quality_threshold": 0.95, "max_iterations": 50}).
Save an entity for the user.

get.description: List a user's saves, newest first. Optionally filter by entity_type.

schema.title: Response List Saves Api Users  User Id  Saves Get

post.description:
Hybrid search across venues and events.

Venues: Qdrant-native search (BM25-only for short queries, BM25+dense
hybrid with server-side RRF for longer/descriptive queries).

Events: Postgres keyword search with pg_trgm similarity scoring.

Results from both sources are merged using Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

get.description:
Fast typeahead autocomplete for the search bar.

Returns suggestions across 5 entity types (venues, events, sites,
neighborhoods, destinations) ranked by pg_trgm similarity.
Designed for <100ms latency.

Find venues and sites near a geographic point.

Uses PostGIS ST_DWithin with GiST index — typically <30ms.

post.description:
Generate (or fetch cached) a Take for an entity.

Phase 1: venues, researching mode, sync response.
Phase 2: adds reviewing_plan mode + trip context.

get.description: Fetch a Take by ID. Used for reloading a specific cached Take.
List all trips for a user (via membership).

schema.title: Response List User Trips Api Users  User Id  Trips Get

get.description: Get a single trip by ID.

patch.description:
Update user-editable trip settings.

Any combination of ``title``, ``place_id``, ``start_date``, ``end_date``,
``occasion``, ``budget_band``, and ``voting_enabled`` may be updated.
Omitted fields are left unchanged (standard PATCH semantics — only fields
present in the request body are applied).

Only trip organizers may call this endpoint. Members who want to propose
changes should do so through the concierge conversation.

Internal fields (``status``, ``trip_summary``, ``planning_brief``) are
managed by background jobs and the concierge, and are not accepted here.

post.description:
Create a new trip in ideation status.

The creator is automatically added as an organizer so they have
authority to finalize proposals, edit trip shape, etc.

Archive a trip and return its conversation to drafting.

The two-way door from promotion. Trip row stays (status='archived')
for audit / undo. The primary conversation is unlinked and its
intent_state.phase set back to 'drafting' so the agent resumes
exploration without losing history.

Only trip members can decommit. The trip doesn't have to be 'ideation' —
later statuses can also be decommitted (e.g. user changed their mind
after confirming).

Email the most recent daily digest for this trip to the caller.

On-demand only in this phase — we don't push digests on a
schedule because the cadence decision (daily? weekly? per-trip?)
is a product question we haven't resolved. This endpoint lets a
user pull their digest to their inbox whenever they want, so the
digest engine's output actually reaches them.

Gates:
- Actor must be a trip member (existing ``_require_trip_member``)
- Actor's ``email`` must be non-null (from Clerk)
- At least one daily digest must exist (the engine populates these
  via the ``_run_digest_loop`` background task)

get.description:
Get all daily digests for a trip, ordered by day number.

Returns the AI-generated day summaries that power the Memory screen.
Only includes ``daily`` digest type — not trip_summary or planning_context.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Digests Api Trips  Trip Id  Digests Get

post.description:
Record where this trip is based, for a given date range.

Trip-member-gated — anyone on the trip can declare the base,
matching existing trip-scoped auth patterns. The concierge will
call this (or a tool backed by it) when the user answers
"where are you staying?" on promotion.

get.description: List all stays for a trip. Default is active only.

schema.title: Response List Trip Accommodations Api Trips  Trip Id  Accommodations Get

delete.description: Retire an active stay (no replacement). Idempotent.

get.description:
Get the latest itinerary with all days and blocks.

Returns a flat list of days, each with embedded blocks, matching
the mobile app's expected shape.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Itinerary Api Trips  Trip Id  Itinerary Get

patch.description:
Partial-update a single itinerary block.

Only the fields present in the request body are updated; null fields
in the body are ignored (use explicit empty-string or zero where needed).
Logs the edit to ``itinerary_edit_log`` for preference inference.

post.description:
Pin an experience block to a specific day of the trip itinerary.

Returns the newly created itinerary block in the same shape as
``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary`` blocks, so the frontend can
append it to the local cache without a full refetch.

get.description:
Return recent itinerary edit log entries for a trip, newest-first.

Each entry records what changed (edit_type), which block was affected,
who made the change, and old/new field snapshots. Used by the concierge
to explain changes and by the preference engine (CIPHER) for inference.

schema.title: Response Get Itinerary Edit History Endpoint Api Trips  Trip Id  Itinerary Edit History Get

get.description:
List experiences for this trip's destination, defaulting to the trip date window.

Convenience wrapper over ``GET /api/experiences`` that resolves the trip's
``place_id`` automatically and pre-filters by trip dates so callers don't
need to look up the destination separately.

Pass ``ranked=true`` to sort by taste-DNA relevance (E-1). Each result will
include a ``why_for_you`` rationale string derived from the user's taste
profile (E-2). Falls back to chronological order when no Personal Memory
exists or when the Qdrant search returns no results.

parameters.name: ranked

schema.description: When true, sort results by taste-DNA relevance using the caller's Personal Memory. Each result includes a why_for_you rationale. Falls back to chronological order if no Personal Memory exists. Ignored when query is set.
schema.title: Ranked

parameters.description: When true, sort results by taste-DNA relevance using the caller's Personal Memory. Each result includes a why_for_you rationale. Falls back to chronological order if no Personal Memory exists. Ignored when query is set.

post.description:
Record a traveler debrief and queue a post-trip reflection into Personal Memory.

Persists a ``trip_debrief_submitted`` user event (trip-scoped) and runs the
same reflection pass as automatic trip completion, with the debrief text
injected into the prompt so it cannot be missed by cursor timing.

get.description:
Get the AI-generated retrospective for a completed trip.

Returns ``null`` when the trip summary has not been generated yet
(e.g. the trip is still in progress, or completed too recently for the
background job to have run). Clients should handle null gracefully.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Summary Endpoint Api Trips  Trip Id  Summary Get

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's full Me-tab profile.

Aggregates: user row + latest personal memory taste dimensions +
active hard constraints count + trip count.

patch.description:
Update the user's editable profile fields (display_name, email).

Returns the updated row. Raises 404 if the user disappears between
auth resolution and the write (essentially impossible) and 409 if
the new email collides with another user.

post.description:
Set or change the user's phone number.

Clears any prior verification state — changing the number requires
a fresh verification before SMS will be delivered. Pass
``phone_number=null`` to remove the number entirely.

Generate a fresh OTP and send it via SMS.

Subsequent calls within the 10-minute expiry invalidate the prior
code (fresh OTP). No rate limit beyond what Twilio enforces —
worth adding server-side throttling when real usage warrants.
Submit the OTP to mark the phone number as verified.

Register (or refresh) a push token for the authenticated user.

Upserts on the token — the same physical device re-registering just
bumps ``last_seen_at`` and re-enables the row. Use this at app
start and after the OS rotates the token.

get.description: List push devices for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Devices Endpoint Api Users  User Id  Devices Get

delete.description: Hard-delete a device registration (e.g. on user logout).

get.description:
Return the user's ambient home conversation, creating on miss.

The home conversation is the trip-less chat where the concierge
relationship lives between trips — inspiration, cross-trip questions,
late-night musings. One per user; bootstrapped at user creation.
This endpoint also backfills for any user who predates the bootstrap.
Return active hard constraints for the user, ordered by type.

schema.title: Response List Constraints Api Users  User Id  Constraints Get

post.description:
Add or reactivate a hard constraint.

Idempotent: re-adding an existing constraint flips ``is_active=true``
and updates severity rather than raising.

delete.description: Deactivate a constraint. Returns 404 if no active row matches.

post.description:
Bulk-submit chip intake answers as hard constraints.

Returns the user's full active constraint list after the write so the
client can render current state without a follow-up GET.

Fires onboarding Personal Memory synthesis as a background task so
the concierge has a rich profile from the first conversation turn.

schema.title: Response Submit Intake Api Users  User Id  Intake Post

delete.description:
Erase everything the concierge has *learned* about the user.

Trips, messages, expenses, hard constraints, follows, and saves are
all preserved. Only synthesized + observed preference data is wiped.
See ``backend.core.db.traveler.reset_personal_data`` for the full
list of tables touched.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's latest Personal Memory document.
Return stored privacy levels. Missing dimensions fall back to client defaults.

schema.title: Response List Privacy Api Users  User Id  Privacy Get

get.description:
Return the agent's current structured facts about this traveler.

Facts are grouped by namespace (``trait``, ``visit``, ``home``, etc.)
and include the confidence score and source evidence the agent recorded.

These facts feed into Personal Memory synthesis and group profile
generation. Users can inspect them to understand what the concierge
knows, and retire incorrect facts via
``DELETE /api/users/{user_id}/facts/{fact_id}``.

Return the full write history for the key belonging to a given fact.

Useful for seeing how a fact evolved over time — e.g., home base
changed from Chicago to New York, or crowd-tolerance was revised
after a new observation.

schema.title: Response Get User Fact History Api Users  User Id  Facts  Fact Id  History Get

delete.description:
Retire an incorrect fact.

Marks the fact as no longer active (stamps ``valid_to``) without
replacing it. The next Personal Memory synthesis cycle will produce
a document that no longer reflects this fact.

Use this when the concierge has recorded something wrong — e.g.,
it inferred the wrong home city or mislabeled a personality trait.
If the fact is *outdated* rather than *wrong*, the concierge will
supersede it automatically when it gathers a correcting observation.

get.description: Return a one-line insight extracted from the user's Personal Memory.

Return a personalized list of venue recommendations.

Scoring pipeline:
  1. Collect published angles, filter excluded IDs, take top 30 by distinctiveness
  2. LLM personalization rerank (Haiku, angle_rank.py)
  3. Engagement stats from angle_user_engagement materialized view
  4. LLM cold prior for angles with < MIN_IMPRESSIONS_FOR_WARM impressions
  5. Three-way blend: 40% LLM + 40% engagement + 20% editorial
  6. Lens-diversity injection (max 2 consecutive same-lens)
  7. Return top 8

``exclude_angle_ids`` — filtered before personalization to skip angles
already shown this session.

schema.title: Response Get For You Api Users  User Id  For You Get

get.description: Return recent trip highlights for the user, ordered by start_date desc.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Highlights Api Users  User Id  Trip Highlights Get

get.description:
Return lean venue detail for the venue detail screen.

Joins ``venues`` → ``places`` (for neighborhood name) and ``briefs``
(for curator content). Returns 404 when the venue ID does not exist.

post.description:
Generate a LiveKit access token for voice chat.

The client uses this token to connect to a LiveKit room via WebRTC.
The voice agent worker automatically joins when a participant connects.

Returns 503 if voice is not configured (no API keys in .env).

Record a geofence trigger and (if applicable) open a session.

Auto-session semantics:
  - On ``entered`` AND no active session → open one anchored at the
    triggering entity and bind the new event row to it via session_id.
  - On any other event type, or when a session is already open →
    record the event with the current session_id (when present);
    do not open or close anything.

The auto-open behavior keeps the client flow tight: a fresh geofence
enter → one HTTP call → client knows both the event id and the
session id. Saves the round-trip vs separate "create session" + "record event."

get.description:
List geofence events for the trip, newest first.

Open to any trip member by design — the organizer-facing dashboard
is the primary consumer and they need the cross-member view to debug
"did the geofence fire for anyone?" Per-user privacy concerns are
deferred to the privacy_audit subsystem; geofence triggers themselves
are coarse-grained and the trip is a shared context.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Filter to one traveler's events. Defaults to all members — the organizer-facing observability surface usually wants the cross-member view to spot 'did anyone reach this stop?'
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Filter to one traveler's events. Defaults to all members — the organizer-facing observability surface usually wants the cross-member view to spot 'did anyone reach this stop?'

parameters.name: event_type

schema.description: Filter to specific event types. Repeat the param for multiple values. Useful for debug ('only show dwell_threshold').
schema.title: Event Type

parameters.description: Filter to specific event types. Repeat the param for multiple values. Useful for debug ('only show dwell_threshold').

schema.title: Response List Geofence Events Endpoint Api Trips  Trip Id  Geofence Events Get

get.description:
Return the caller's currently-active session, or 404 if none.

Scoped to the caller — there's deliberately no "get someone else's
session" path. Sessions are personal narrative state, not group
state. Cross-member visibility lands at the geofence-event layer
instead.

post.description:
Close the caller's active session manually ("end guide" tap).

Idempotent — calling close on an already-closed session returns the
existing row unmodified (per ``close_session`` semantics). Returns
404 if no session has ever been opened for this user on this trip.

get.description:
Return TTFT percentiles + per-persona breakdown for the window.

Computes p50 / p95 / p99 / mean / stddev for both ``ttft_ms`` and
``total_agent_latency_ms``, plus a per-persona slice ranked by
sample size so the noisy long-tail personas don't dominate the
response payload.

parameters.name: date_from

schema.description: ISO8601 lower bound on ``created_at`` (inclusive). Default: now - 24h. Aware datetimes are honored as-is; naive inputs are treated as UTC.
schema.title: Date From

parameters.description: ISO8601 lower bound on ``created_at`` (inclusive). Default: now - 24h. Aware datetimes are honored as-is; naive inputs are treated as UTC.

parameters.name: date_to

schema.description: ISO8601 upper bound on ``created_at`` (inclusive). Default: now.
schema.title: Date To

parameters.description: ISO8601 upper bound on ``created_at`` (inclusive). Default: now.

parameters.name: stt_provider

schema.description: Scope to one STT provider (e.g. 'deepgram_cloud', 'apple_speech_analyzer', 'picovoice_cheetah'). Useful for comparing the on-device fallback's latency profile against the cloud baseline.
schema.title: Stt Provider

parameters.description: Scope to one STT provider (e.g. 'deepgram_cloud', 'apple_speech_analyzer', 'picovoice_cheetah'). Useful for comparing the on-device fallback's latency profile against the cloud baseline.

post.description:
Open a voice session bound to the interrupted narration's context.

Auth: requesting user must be a trip member AND must be the user
in the request body (no on-behalf-of).

Resolves the narration cache row to compute heard/remaining text
via Phase 2.1 word-level timestamps. When timestamps aren't
available (legacy rows pre-Phase-2.1), falls back to "user heard
everything" — the bridge generator will phrase the resume
accordingly.

Render the bridge sentence to MP3 via the persona's TTS voice.

Auth: trip membership. The bridge endpoint is part of the voice
flow which requires Voice keys configured (Cartesia for v1).

Webhook from Bland.ai when a voice call completes.

Updates the restaurant_booking_attempts record with the transcript
and parsed confirmation details.

schema.title: Response Bland Ai Callback Webhooks Bland Ai Call Complete Post

post.description:
Webhook from Twilio when a WhatsApp/SMS reply is received.

Parses the reply and updates the restaurant_booking_attempts record.

schema.title: Response Twilio Message Callback Webhooks Twilio Message Reply Post

get.description: Basic liveness check — always returns 200 if the process is running.

Readiness check — verifies Postgres and Qdrant are reachable.

Returns 200 with component status if all healthy, 503 if any are down.
Use this for K8s readiness probes / Cloud Run health checks.

Report status of all background asyncio tasks.

Returns per-task status: running | disabled | done | cancelled | error.
Useful for confirming that DISABLE_LLM_BACKGROUND_LOOPS is working or
that a task hasn't silently crashed.

schema.title: Response Background Task Health Health Background Tasks Get

get.description: Return cumulative LLM token usage and estimated cost.

schema.title: Response Token Usage Debug Tokens Get

get.description:
Return a per-day LLM cost breakdown for the last ``days`` days.

In-memory aggregate — resets on process restart. For historical
analysis, query the ``prompt_calls`` table directly.

schema.title: Response Cost Breakdown Debug Cost Get

AccommodationWriteRequest.title: AccommodationWriteRequest
AccommodationWriteRequest.description:
Create or replace a trip accommodation.

Exactly one of ``accommodation_id`` or ``(freeform_lat, freeform_lng)``
must be provided. The server-side Pydantic validator and DB CHECK
both enforce this — the client gets a 422 or 400 on inconsistency.

AlternativeOption.title: AlternativeOption
AlternativeOption.description:
One alternative option a proposal may offer alongside the primary pick.

``extra="allow"`` keeps legacy fields (e.g. site_id, experience_id)
readable for older proposals.

AngleDetailAngle.title: AngleDetailAngle
AngleDetailAngle.description: Full angle payload — preview fields + long-form dossier body.

AngleMember.title: AngleMember
AngleMember.description:
A single exemplar venue of an angle, joined with venue display fields
for API responses. The shape the browse surface actually renders.

AnglePreview.title: AnglePreview
AnglePreview.description:
List-shape — what appears on a Discover row or a venue breadcrumb.

``title`` resolves server-side to ``display_title or title`` so clients
never need to know the distinction between the research hypothesis
title and the editorial display title.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the image to rehost.

curator_ref.title: Curator Ref
curator_ref.description: Curator identifier, e.g. 'curator:alex'. Free-form for now.

AuditEvent.title: AuditEvent
AuditEvent.description: One audit-trail entry. Designed to be human-readable in a settings UI.

BlockTransition.title: BlockTransition
BlockTransition.description:
How the group moves from this block to the next.

``mode`` + ``minutes`` are the canonical pair; ``rationale`` is the
one-line "why this way" that lets the UI explain pacing choices.
``distance_km`` is optional — the planner emits it when it's useful
context (long taxi, cross-city train). Booking-specific keys
(``origin``, ``destination``, ``pickup_location``) still live here as
extras so the booking agent's existing readers keep working.

BridgeRenderRequest.title: BridgeRenderRequest
BridgeRenderRequest.description: Render a bridge sentence to audio for inline playback.

BridgeRenderResponse.title: BridgeRenderResponse
BridgeRenderResponse.description:
Inline base64 audio data URL for the bridge.

Phase 6b.3 v1 returns the audio inline so the client can hand
it directly to ``adapter.setSource()`` without an extra round-trip.
Phase 8 can switch to CDN URLs once R2 is wired and bridge audio
starts being cached server-side.

Caveat.title: Caveat
Caveat.description:
Structured because caveats get special UI treatment on the phone —
they can't be buried in the body (allergen warnings, accessibility, etc.).

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier from the client

CollectionDetail.title: CollectionDetail
CollectionDetail.description: Full collection payload: metadata + ordered hydrated members.

CollectionSummary.title: CollectionSummary
CollectionSummary.description: Guides-row card payload: just what the row needs to render.

ConversationListItem.title: ConversationListItem
ConversationListItem.description:
Lightweight conversation for list views.

Enriched fields are populated by ``get_conversation_list_for_user``
so the history surface can render last-message preview + unread
count without follow-up fetches per row. Older clients that don't
need the enrichment still receive the legacy shape — the new
fields default to None / 0 so a Pydantic decode against existing
snapshots stays happy.

CreateSideChatRequest.title: CreateSideChatRequest
CreateSideChatRequest.description: Create a personal side conversation linked to a trip.

CreateSideChatResponse.title: CreateSideChatResponse
CreateSideChatResponse.description: Response after creating a side chat.

DeepResearchRequest.title: DeepResearchRequest
DeepResearchRequest.description: Request to run a deep research pass (background, minutes).

DigestRetryRequest.title: DigestRetryRequest
DigestRetryRequest.description:
Targeted retry for trips whose background digest failed.

``trip_ids`` is the list of UUIDs returned by
``run_digest_for_active_trips`` in its ``failed_trip_ids`` field —
typically surfaced in the warning log and passed directly here by ops.

``date`` overrides the target date; defaults to yesterday (the same
date the background loop would have used).  Pass an ISO date string
if you need to backfill a specific day.

DigestRunResponse.title: DigestRunResponse
DigestRunResponse.description: Result of a manual digest run.

EntityTake.title: EntityTake
EntityTake.description: A cached Take row — what the DB stores and the API returns.

ExpenseShareInput.title: ExpenseShareInput
ExpenseShareInput.description: Used in ExpenseCreate/Update to define shares inline.

GeofenceEvent.title: GeofenceEvent
GeofenceEvent.description:
One geofence trigger the server observed.

Append-only: every row represents a moment in time, immutable after
insert. ``manually_narrated`` is the catch-all for cases where the
user tapped the entity card without ever entering its geofence — we
still record it so the observability dashboard reflects total
narration attempts, geofence-driven or not.

HandoffNarrationContext.title: HandoffNarrationContext
HandoffNarrationContext.description:
The context block the voice agent reads before its first turn.

Includes what the user heard, what was about to play, and the
trigger transcript — enough to ground the agent's reply without
a separate context fetch.

HeadingBlock.title: HeadingBlock
HeadingBlock.description:
A section heading. ``level`` is 2 or 3 — we deliberately don't
expose h1 because the Take headline is the de facto h1 of the card.

active_in_chat.title: Active In Chat
active_in_chat.description: Is the chat screen foregrounded right now?

headphones_connected.title: Headphones Connected
headphones_connected.description: Any audio output that's not the phone speaker?

motion.title: Motion
motion.description: Coarse activity: still / walking / running / cycling / driving / unknown

ambient_db.title: Ambient Db
ambient_db.description: Optional ambient noise estimate in dB

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the user is currently engaged with, if any.

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier; required when trip_id is set.

HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest
HeartbeatRequest.description:
Compact physical-context signal from the client.

All fields optional — clients may have permission for some signals
but not others (e.g., motion without microphone for ambient_db).

HydratedMember.title: HydratedMember
HydratedMember.description:
One member with its entity type tag + lightweight entity payload.

The entity payload is shaped by the DB layer; the shape varies per
entity type (a venue has `name`, a dossier has `angle`, etc.). The
mobile app switches on `entity_type` to render.

Hypothesis.title: Hypothesis
Hypothesis.description:
Internal-only. The LLM's working claim about the user and its evidence.

NOT surfaced to the user. Used for:
- Debugging + eval ("why did the Take emphasize X?")
- Offline analysis (aggregating which hypothesis categories correlate
  with saves vs dismisses, feeding Personal Memory refresh)
- Grounding validation (post-generation check that every hypothesis
  references real evidence)

`evidence_refs` are opaque ID strings scoped by `evidence_type`:
- personal_memory: the Personal Memory version id or a section marker
- past_trip: trip_id
- angle_affinity: angle_id
- group_signal: group_profile_version id
- saved_entity: entity_id previously saved

IntakePayload.title: IntakePayload
IntakePayload.description:
Bulk chip payload for minimal onboarding.

Each field is optional. Sections use replace-within-section semantics:
submitting ``dietary=["vegan"]`` deactivates any other active dietary rows
for the user. Omitting a field leaves that section untouched, so callers
can update one section without disturbing others. An empty list clears
the section.

entity_id.title: Entity Id
entity_id.description: The narrated entity's id (str or uuid)

playback_position_ms.title: Playback Position Ms
playback_position_ms.description: Position into the audio when the event fired. Null for dismissed/interrupted.

trip_phase.title: Trip Phase
trip_phase.description: pre_trip | during_trip | post_trip — set by client when known

audio_position_ms.title: Audio Position Ms
audio_position_ms.description: Where in the audio the user was when they interrupted.

trigger_transcript.title: Trigger Transcript
trigger_transcript.description: The user's speech that triggered the interrupt — handed to the voice agent so it can respond directly to what was asked.

InterruptedNarrationContext.title: InterruptedNarrationContext
InterruptedNarrationContext.description: What the client knows about the narration that was interrupted.

InviteConversationSummary.title: InviteConversationSummary
InviteConversationSummary.description:
Landing-page facts for a conversation-scoped invite (pre-trip).

Lighter than :class:`InviteTripSummary` — the conversation is still
exploratory, so we render what the intent scratchpad has captured
so far rather than committed trip details.

InviteIntakeRequest.title: InviteIntakeRequest
InviteIntakeRequest.description: Chip answers and optional contact info captured pre-auth.

InviteListItem.title: InviteListItem
InviteListItem.description:
Serializable summary of one invite row, returned by list endpoints.

Used by the organizer's "Pending invites" UI — each item carries
enough state to render a status pill (sent / answered / joined /
revoked / expired) and the small momentum signal of a chip answer
when the recipient has already engaged pre-auth.

InviteSnapshot.title: InviteSnapshot
InviteSnapshot.description: Haiku-generated landing-page content for one invite view.

InviteTripSummary.title: InviteTripSummary
InviteTripSummary.description: Non-generated facts shown on the landing page alongside the snapshot.

ItineraryBlockOut.title: ItineraryBlockOut
ItineraryBlockOut.description: API response shape for a single itinerary block.

ItineraryDayOut.title: ItineraryDayOut
ItineraryDayOut.description: API response shape for a single itinerary day with embedded blocks.

LatencyDistribution.title: LatencyDistribution
LatencyDistribution.description:
Percentile + spread summary for one latency series.

All values are integer milliseconds — the underlying column is an
INTEGER and we explicitly cast back so a float-formatted ``stddev``
doesn't leak into the response (would surprise frontend code that
keys off integer ms throughout).

LeaseInfo.title: LeaseInfo
LeaseInfo.description: Common shape for claim / get_active responses.

LensGroup.title: LensGroup
LensGroup.description: All published angles under one travel lens, for the editorial index view.

ListBlock.title: ListBlock
ListBlock.description: An ordered or bulleted list. Each item supports inline markdown.

LocationReading.title: LocationReading
LocationReading.description:
A single GPS reading from a client device.

Written to ``conversations.metadata_.last_location`` and read by
the concierge's origin resolver (15-min TTL). Piggybacks on message
send requests so the concierge always sees the freshest available
location at turn time without a separate round-trip.

ManifestEntry.title: ManifestEntry
ManifestEntry.description:
One entry in the offline-narration manifest.

Carries everything the client needs to render the entry (entity
metadata, depth tier) plus the audio URL to download. The
``audio_url`` is a relative path against the API base — the client
constructs the absolute URL itself so this works across dev /
staging / prod without server-side host detection.

MemoryResetResponse.title: MemoryResetResponse
MemoryResetResponse.description: Counts of rows deleted per source table — useful for confirmation UI.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type — Anthropic vision accepts JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF.

MessageImage.title: MessageImage
MessageImage.description:
Inline image attached to a chat message.

The Anthropic chat API accepts image content blocks in messages —
we forward the base64 + mime type through the agent loop so the
concierge can see what the user is asking about ("what is this
monument?", "is this dish vegetarian?", scan a paper menu).

Sized for mobile: clients should compress to <=1MB base64 (typical
JPEG at 1920px wide, quality 0.7 = 150-300KB). Larger images flow
through the existing receipt-upload path instead.

Mirrors the receipt-OCR call shape (``backend/expenses/receipt_ocr.py``)
so the same vision-capable code path serves both surfaces.

persona_override.title: Persona Override
persona_override.description: Optional override for the persona auto-resolved from the trip's destination. Eval/admin only — production clients should leave this null.

location.description: Optional GPS reading from the client at trigger time.

session_arc.title: Session Arc
session_arc.description: Legacy ephemeral arc text. Kept for backward compatibility; new clients should pass ``session_id`` instead and let the server load the arc from ``voice_guide_sessions``. When both are set, ``session_id`` wins — the persistent arc is the source of truth.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Optional voice-guide session this narration belongs to. When set and the session is open, the server uses the session's ``arc_summary`` as the prompt context (overriding any request-supplied ``session_arc``) and appends a one-line entry after a successful narration so the next call sees this stop in the arc. Best-effort throughout — a missing or closed session falls back to the legacy ``session_arc`` behavior without surfacing an error.

NarrateRequest.title: NarrateRequest
NarrateRequest.description: A geofence-triggered or 'I'm at X' arrival event.

NarrationEntity.title: NarrationEntity
NarrationEntity.description:
The place being narrated.

``id`` is a string at the wire level so UUID-keyed entities (experiences)
and integer-keyed entities (venues, sites, places) can share the
schema. Server-side coercion to int happens before retrieval — see
``_coerce_entity_id`` below.

NarrationManifest.title: NarrationManifest
NarrationManifest.description:
Manifest of all pre-rendered narration audio for a trip.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.5. The client fetches this on a WiFi
connection pre-departure to download every cached narration to
local storage. Once downloaded, ``useNarrationAudio`` plays from
the local path when offline.

NarrationStop.title: NarrationStop
NarrationStop.description: One navigable stop in the trip's itinerary, with geofence metadata.

NearbyItem.title: NearbyItem
NearbyItem.description: A venue or site near a given point.

NearbyResponse.title: NearbyResponse
NearbyResponse.description: Nearby venues/sites around a geographic point.

NotificationStatsResponse.title: NotificationStatsResponse
NotificationStatsResponse.description: Aggregated engagement metrics for a trip's notification outcomes.

ParagraphBlock.title: ParagraphBlock
ParagraphBlock.description: A prose paragraph. Supports inline markdown in ``text``.

PatchTripRequest.title: PatchTripRequest
PatchTripRequest.description:
User-editable trip fields.

Excludes internal fields (``status``, ``trip_summary``, ``planning_brief``)
that are managed by the concierge or background jobs and must not be
overwritten by direct client mutations.

PendingInviteAnswer.title: PendingInviteAnswer
PendingInviteAnswer.description:
An invite the organizer hasn't acknowledged yet, where the recipient
left a pre-auth chip answer.

Surfaces "Sam answered: 'I'm in'" in the bell. Cleared when the
organizer opens ``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/invites`` (the Pending
invites surface) — that endpoint stamps ``intake_seen_at = now()``
on every invite for the trip.

PendingVote.title: PendingVote
PendingVote.description: A proposal the user hasn't voted on.

PersonaBreakdown.title: PersonaBreakdown
PersonaBreakdown.description:
Per-persona TTFT slice. ``persona_id`` is null for turns that
didn't carry a persona_id (legacy rows; new turns always set it).

PlaceAnglesByLens.title: PlaceAnglesByLens
PlaceAnglesByLens.description:
Response for ``GET /api/places/{slug}/angles/by-lens``.

``lens_groups`` contains one entry per lens that has ≥ 1 published angle.
``unlensed`` holds published angles without a ``travel_lens`` value (older
content or user-query angles that weren't assigned one).

PrerenderHintResponse.title: PrerenderHintResponse
PrerenderHintResponse.description: Response shape for the speculative pre-render endpoint.

ProactiveNotification.title: ProactiveNotification
ProactiveNotification.description:
A proactive in-app notification from the notification intelligence system.

Sourced from ``notification_deliveries`` (channel='in_app') joined with
``notification_outcomes`` so the feed can surface personalised nudges
alongside conversation unread counts and proposal votes.

force.title: Force
force.description: Override the readiness gate. By default we require at least one destination and a date window in the intent scratchpad; set force=True to promote anyway (e.g. for testing).

PromoteToTripRequest.title: PromoteToTripRequest
PromoteToTripRequest.description: Explicit promotion of a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

ProposalDetail.title: ProposalDetail
ProposalDetail.description:
Full proposal detail.

When ``proposed_entity_type`` + ``proposed_entity_id`` are set, the
mobile app deep-links the proposal card into that entity's detail
view, where a per-viewer personalized Take is fetched via
``POST /api/takes``. Both are null for proposals that don't point
at a single entity (e.g. pure reschedule, "add a day").

``voting_enabled`` mirrors the trip-level flag — the mobile app uses
it to decide whether to render the vote widget (enabled) or a
reaction-only view with an "ask organizer to resolve" affordance
(disabled).

ProposalImpactAnalysis.title: ProposalImpactAnalysis
ProposalImpactAnalysis.description:
Structured trade-off payload for a change proposal.

All fields are optional so the concierge can fill in as much or as
little as warranted by the proposal stakes. ``extra="allow"`` preserves
legacy keys (e.g. ``new_time``, ``new_title``, ``new_notes``) written
by older proposals and consumed by ``proposal_apply._apply_*``.

ProposalSummary.title: ProposalSummary
ProposalSummary.description:
Lightweight proposal for list views.

Deliberately omits ``proposed_entity_*`` — the deep-link affordance
is a detail-page concern, not a list-row concern.

ProviderBreakdown.title: ProviderBreakdown
ProviderBreakdown.description:
Per-STT-provider TTFT slice (V-3). Surfaces the latency cost of
the on-device fallback (Apple SpeechAnalyzer / Picovoice) vs. the
cloud baseline (Deepgram). Once the iOS native module ships and
starts emitting ``stt_provider != 'deepgram_cloud'``, this is the
surface that lights up to compare them.

QuickResearchRequest.title: QuickResearchRequest
QuickResearchRequest.description: Request to run a quick research pass (~1 minute).

ReactRequest.title: ReactRequest
ReactRequest.description: React to a structured message (reaction_card or vote_widget).

move.title: Move
move.description: One-line verdict — 'the move is X because Y'. Opinionated.

why_for_group.title: Why For Group
why_for_group.description: Why this fits THIS group (energy, constraints, dynamic).

why_for_person.title: Why For Person
why_for_person.description: Why this fits the requester specifically (personal memory, prior preferences).

what_to_notice.title: What To Notice
what_to_notice.description: Connoisseur notes — what to look for, order, ask about.

what_to_skip.title: What To Skip
what_to_skip.description: What to avoid or deprioritize at this place.

timing_note.title: Timing Note
timing_note.description: When to go, how long, reservation tip, time-of-day cue.

RecommendationPayload.title: RecommendationPayload
RecommendationPayload.description: Structured reasoning attached to a concierge recommendation.

accuracy_m.title: Accuracy M
accuracy_m.description: Reported GPS accuracy in meters; null when client can't supply it.

RecordGeofenceEventRequest.title: RecordGeofenceEventRequest
RecordGeofenceEventRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/geofence-events``.

The ``user_id`` is taken from auth — clients can only record their
own events. Attempting to specify someone else would be a privacy
leak and is rejected at the application layer regardless.

RecordGeofenceEventResponse.title: RecordGeofenceEventResponse
RecordGeofenceEventResponse.description:
Composite response: the event row + the session it's bound to.

When ``event_type='entered'`` and no session is active, the route
opens one and returns it inline so the client doesn't need a second
round-trip to learn its session_id. Otherwise ``session`` is null
(the trigger was recorded but no session changes resulted) or the
existing active session.

RegisterDeviceRequest.title: RegisterDeviceRequest
RegisterDeviceRequest.description: Mobile client calls this on login / app start to register its push token.

ResearchTaskResponse.title: ResearchTaskResponse
ResearchTaskResponse.description: Acknowledgement that a research task has been enqueued.

ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest
ResolveRequest.description:
Resolve (accept/reject) a change proposal.

The resolver is the authenticated actor — they must be an
organizer of the trip. No ``resolved_by`` in the body; trusting
the client to name the resolver would bypass authz.

RevertRequest.title: RevertRequest
RevertRequest.description:
Revert an already-accepted proposal, restoring the prior itinerary state.

Only the trip organizer may revert. The ``resolution_note`` is stored on
the proposal row and surfaced in the group chat bridge message so members
understand why the change was undone.

RouteRequest.title: RouteRequest
RouteRequest.description: Inputs to the modality routing decision.

experience_type.title: Experience Type
experience_type.description: concert, theater, comedy, festival, tour, etc.

entity_types.title: Entity Types
entity_types.description: Entity types to search: ["venue", "experience", "site", "accommodation"]. "event" is accepted as a legacy alias for "experience" during migration. Defaults to all.

SearchRequest.title: SearchRequest
SearchRequest.description: Unified search request — drives the Discover tab search bar.

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description: Unified search response with fused results.

SearchResultItem.title: SearchResultItem
SearchResultItem.description: A single search result — venue, experience, site, or accommodation.

SessionInfoResponse.title: SessionInfoResponse
SessionInfoResponse.description: Basic info about the current session.

SetPhoneRequest.title: SetPhoneRequest
SetPhoneRequest.description: E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234). Pass null to clear.

SpeculativeAngle.title: SpeculativeAngle
SpeculativeAngle.description:
A non-persisted angle generated on the fly from a user query.

These are suggestions only — no ``id``, no dossier.  The frontend can
display them as fast-start discovery chips ("Want to explore this angle?")
and the user can trigger a proper research run from there.

SuggestResponse.title: SuggestResponse
SuggestResponse.description: Autocomplete suggestions for the search bar.

TakeGenerationRequest.title: TakeGenerationRequest
TakeGenerationRequest.description: Body for POST /api/takes — request to generate (or fetch cached) a Take.

body.title: Body
body.description: Ordered list of content blocks. Paragraphs are the default; use headings only when the Take has 3+ distinct sections; use lists when enumerating 3+ items that read poorly as prose.

TakePayload.title: TakePayload
TakePayload.description:
The full Take content — what gets rendered (and what gets logged).

Tier 1 (Curator) and Tier 2 (Personal) share this shape. Personal Takes
include grounded `hypotheses` with real evidence refs; Curator Takes
leave `hypotheses` empty.

TakeResponse.title: TakeResponse
TakeResponse.description: Response shape for non-streaming Take requests.

TripContext.title: TripContext
TripContext.description: Trip-scoped context for filtering and relevance.

TripDebriefSubmit.title: TripDebriefSubmit
TripDebriefSubmit.description: Structured post-trip debrief — funnels explicit traveler signal into memory.

TripDigest.title: TripDigest
TripDigest.description:
A daily, trip-summary, or planning-context structured digest.

`content` is the LLM's structured JSON output; `rendered_markdown` is a
pre-formatted textual view populated at generation time so consumers
(planner prompt, admin UI) don't need to re-render the payload.

TripSummaryResponse.title: TripSummaryResponse
TripSummaryResponse.description: AI-generated retrospective for a completed trip.

UserFact.title: UserFact
UserFact.description:
A single structured fact about a traveler.

Supersession forms a linked list: a fact with ``valid_to`` set AND
``superseded_by`` pointing at a newer fact means "I now know better".
``valid_to`` set with ``superseded_by`` null means "retired without
replacement" (forget_fact).

UserFactsResponse.title: UserFactsResponse
UserFactsResponse.description: Active user facts grouped by namespace, plus the namespace catalog.

UserMeResponse.title: UserMeResponse
UserMeResponse.description: Response for GET /api/me — full profile data for the Me tab.

UserMessageResponse.title: UserMessageResponse
UserMessageResponse.description: Response when a message is persisted without triggering an agent reply.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial update — only include fields you want to change.

VenueDetailResponse.title: VenueDetailResponse
VenueDetailResponse.description:
Shape returned by GET /api/venues/{venue_id}.

Mirrors ``APIVenueDetail`` in the frontend types.

Verdict.title: Verdict
Verdict.description:
Opinion layer. Derived from signals, not free LLM judgment.

`derived_from` lists the signal categories the verdict is grounded in,
for traceability and debugging.

VoiceGuideSession.title: VoiceGuideSession
VoiceGuideSession.description:
One arc the user moved through, anchored at an opening entity.

A session opens when narration starts at a stop and stays open while
the user moves through related stops (same place, same angle ladder)
within a rolling window. ``arc_summary`` is the running text the next
narration call appends to and feeds back to the LLM. ``closed_at``
lands when a new place is entered or the user goes idle past a
threshold.

VoiceHandoffRequest.title: VoiceHandoffRequest
VoiceHandoffRequest.description: Request for an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceHandoffResponse.title: VoiceHandoffResponse
VoiceHandoffResponse.description: Token + context for opening an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceMetricsWindow.title: VoiceMetricsWindow
VoiceMetricsWindow.description:
Echoed-back window + sample size so the caller knows what they
actually got — important when the requested window had no data.

VoicePersonaInfo.title: VoicePersonaInfo
VoicePersonaInfo.description:
Persona metadata surfaced to the client so the UI can render the
persona name + voice provenance during a voice session.

Phase 4.6. The client uses this to show "Talking with Mateus" in the
voice overlay and to know which voice (id) the TTS will use. The
client doesn't need full persona spec — just identity for display.

VoiceTokenRequest.title: VoiceTokenRequest
VoiceTokenRequest.description: Request a LiveKit access token for voice chat.

VoiceTokenResponse.title: VoiceTokenResponse
VoiceTokenResponse.description: LiveKit connection credentials for the client.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Written to conversation metadata before the turn runs; read by the concierge's distance tool when from_origin='current_location'.

images.title: Images
images.description: Optional images to attach to this message. The concierge answers questions about what's in the photo (translate a menu, identify a monument, scan an entry sign). Multiple images are concatenated in order. Limit 4 per message.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: A single message from a user to the concierge.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: What the concierge returns after processing a message.

trigger_agent.title: Trigger Agent
trigger_agent.description: If False, persist the message without triggering an agent reply. Useful for user-to-user chat in group conversations.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Stored on the conversation's metadata before the turn runs.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Send a message in a conversation.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Agent reply after processing a message.
